intro: Syzygium maire, also known as the Tahina or Maire's syzygium, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. The plant is traditionally used in Polynesian medicine to treat various health issues, with reported benefits including antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities that can aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and protect against infections. Syzygium maire contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to reap its health benefits, although modern studies are needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
